one_line_takeaway: FragileFlow adds a spectral-norm regularizer on a novel margin-gated error-flow matrix plus a stability term to existing robust-adaptation objectives (CE+aug, R3F, SMART for LLMs; adversarial LoRA for CLIP), backed by the first PAC-Bayes bound connecting this margin-aware quantity to worst-class robustness, and empirically it reduces the targeted risk measures in essentially every paired comparison across 18 LLM settings and 3 VLM datasets under PGD, though worst-class accuracy gains do not track the risk reduction in every case (e.g., DTD perturbed worst-class stays at 0.00% regardless).
key_finding: On the LLM benchmark suite (Table 1: 3 models x 2 datasets x 3 base learners = 18 paired comparisons at the calibrated buffer gamma_25), FragileFlow reduces both VWR_gamma and VSR_gamma in every single paired comparison versus the matched base learner, and improves perturbed worst-class (Ptb WC) accuracy in most but not all settings while keeping clean accuracy comparable (e.g., largest single WC-Acc gain: Qwen-1.5B/ARC-C CE+aug base 63.84 -> +plug 68.62, a +4.78pp change; Mistral-7B/ARC-C SMART base 26.67 -> +plug 36.09, a +9.42pp change with VWR dropping 52.44->43.60 and VSR 41.88->36.11). On the VLM cross-modal test under PGD-eps=1.0/255 (Table 2, CLIP ViT-B/32 + LoRA on DTD/OxfordPets/Caltech101, 16-shot), FragileFlow (inner/outer/both placements) again lowers VWR_gamma and VSR_gamma versus the no-plug baseline in essentially all dataset/placement combinations while clean accuracy is largely preserved; perturbed worst-class accuracy on DTD stays at 0.00 in every configuration (base and plug), showing the plug-in does not fix catastrophic worst-class collapse in that setting even though it reduces the risk measures. The mechanistic ablation (Table 3) isolates R_spec vs R_spec+beta*R_stab: on LLMs, R_spec alone gives -13.02% VSR / -13.14% VWR / +0.48pp worst-class accuracy, while adding R_stab strengthens this to -13.97% / -14.37% / +1.52pp WC (+0.68pp perturbed-avg-acc, +0.57pp clean-acc); on VLMs, R_spec alone gives -5.21% VSR / -3.35% VWR / +0.24pp WC, and the composite gives -6.47% / -4.35% / +0.28pp WC. Figure 3 shows Pearson correlations of ~0.88-0.97 between sigma_max (VSR) and normalized VWR across settings, and that the plug-in's operating-point centroid moves toward lower-left (lower risk) in both spectral-only and composite variants.
core_contribution: FragileFlow is a plug-in regularizer for robust adaptation of LLMs/VLMs on finite-option (multiple-choice / classification) tasks. It (1) formalizes the 'correct-but-fragile' failure mode -- a prediction stays correct while probability mass already leaks toward specific wrong competitors near the decision boundary -- via a margin-aware error-flow matrix built with a calibrated margin gate g_gamma,kappa (a smoothed indicator of examples inside a safety buffer gamma around the margin); (2) defines two risk measures from this matrix, the vulnerable worst-option risk VWR_gamma (matrix 1-norm) and vulnerable spectral risk VSR_gamma (spectral norm), and shows VWR_gamma <= sqrt(K) * VSR_gamma; (3) proves what the authors state is the first PAC-Bayes upper bound relating an empirical spectral error-flow term (measured on a perturbed sample S') to the population vulnerable worst-option risk under randomized (Gibbs) adaptation (Theorem 2.10); (4) bridges this posterior-averaged bound to the deterministic worst-class risk actually used at test time via a logit-stability condition (Definition 2.12, Proposition 2.13); and (5) instantiates the theory as a practical training objective L_total = L_base + alpha*R_spec + beta*R_stab, where R_spec is a mini-batch spectral-norm penalty on the margin-aware error-flow matrix and R_stab is a stop-gradient KL term penalizing prediction changes under joint input+coordinate perturbation. FragileFlow is designed to attach to existing adaptation objectives (CE+augmentation, R3F, SMART for LLMs; inner/outer PGD steps of adversarial LoRA adaptation for CLIP) rather than replace them.
threat_model: Bounded input perturbations, not a fully adaptive white-box attacker optimizing against the defense end-to-end. For LLMs (Qwen2.5-0.5B/1.5B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2 on ARC-Challenge and CommonsenseQA), the perturbation set U(x) consists of task-preserving text perturbations -- typo noise, distractor insertion, and format rewriting -- drawn from a perturbation rule Pi(.|x,y) that the paper states 'may represent either random or adversarially selected perturbations' (Definition 2.3), but the concrete text perturbations evaluated are fixed, non-adaptive rewrite rules rather than gradient-based attacks against the model. For VLMs (CLIP ViT-B/32 with LoRA adaptation on DTD, OxfordPets, Caltech101), the threat model is a genuine gradient-based adversary: robustness is evaluated under 100-step PGD at epsilon = 1.0/255 on input images, following an adversarial LoRA adaptation protocol (Ghiasvand et al.). The paper's goal is not to prevent any single misclassification but to bound the worst-class deterministic error rate (WCR_det) -- i.e., prevent any one true class from having disproportionately high adversarial/perturbed error -- rather than only improving average accuracy under perturbation.
limitations: The paper itself flags: (1) in a subset of paired comparisons, perturbed worst-class accuracy does not increase even though both risk measures decrease, showing the theoretical risk reduction does not always convert to the deterministic downstream metric -- attributed to the stability bridge (Proposition 2.13) and base-learner optimization behavior, not a guaranteed transfer; (2) the PAC-Bayes bound (Theorem 2.10) is stated for a randomized Gibbs posterior Q, while the deployed model is the deterministic mean model theta(mu); the connection requires an additional logit-stability assumption (Definition 2.12) that is not directly verified/measured empirically in the reported experiments, only motivated via the R_stab proxy; (3) the buffer gamma is not a fixed universal constant but a per-setting empirical quantile (q=0.25) calibrated on a held-out perturbed validation split, so results depend on this calibration choice, and the paper only sweeps q in {0.10,0.25,0.50}; (4) LLM perturbations (typo noise, distractor insertion, format rewriting) are fixed, non-adaptive perturbation rules, not attacks that adapt to the defense, so robustness claims for LLMs do not cover an adaptive/optimization-based adversary the way the PGD evaluation does for VLMs; (5) DTD worst-class accuracy under PGD remains at 0.00% for both base and plug-in in Table 2, i.e. the method does not solve worst-class catastrophic failure in that dataset even though it reduces the proposed risk measures; (6) the paper's own conclusion states an open next step ('currently pursuing') to understand whether the regularizer suppresses fragile representation directions internally or extends to generated tokens/free-form generation -- the current work is restricted to finite-option (multiple-choice/classification) prediction interfaces only.

quantitative_results: LLM Table 1, Qwen-0.5B/ARC-C: CE+aug base->plug VWR 48.07->38.46, VSR 43.34->34.26, Ptb-WC-Acc 38.58->38.31 (essentially flat), Clean-Acc 54.20->53.87
LLM Table 1, Qwen-1.5B/ARC-C: CE+aug base->plug Ptb-WC-Acc 63.84->68.62 (+4.78pp), VWR 30.34->26.46, Clean-Acc 76.20->76.93
LLM Table 1, Mistral-7B/ARC-C: SMART base->plug VWR 52.44->43.60, VSR 41.88->36.11, Ptb-WC-Acc 26.67->36.09 (+9.42pp), Clean-Acc 68.67->71.73
VLM Table 2, DTD (no-plug vs Both placement): VWR 28.33->26.89, VSR 38.97->36.15, Clean-Acc 67.57->67.38, Ptb-Acc 22.66->22.28, Ptb-WC-Acc stays 0.00 in all configurations
VLM Table 2, OxfordPets (no-plug vs Both): VWR 63.53->61.53, VSR 58.81->56.82, Clean-WC 54.00->56.33, Ptb-WC 0.00->0.33
VLM Table 2, Caltech101 (no-plug vs Inner): VWR 47.30->45.40, VSR 22.20->20.79, Ptb-WC stays 5.19->5.19
Ablation Table 3, LLM, R_spec only (beta=0): VSR -13.02%, VWR -13.14%, WC +0.48pp, PtbAcc +0.44pp, CleanAcc +0.42pp
Ablation Table 3, LLM, R_spec+beta*R_stab (composite/main): VSR -13.97%, VWR -14.37%, WC +1.52pp, PtbAcc +0.68pp, CleanAcc +0.57pp
Ablation Table 3, VLM, R_spec only: VSR -5.21%, VWR -3.35%, WC +0.24pp, PtbAcc +0.33pp, CleanAcc -0.08pp
Ablation Table 3, VLM, composite: VSR -6.47%, VWR -4.35%, WC +0.28pp, PtbAcc +0.28pp, CleanAcc +0.02pp
Norm relation: VWR_gamma(theta;D') <= sqrt(K) * VSR_gamma(theta;D') (Appendix A.2)
PGD attack setting for VLM eval: 100-step PGD, epsilon = 1.0/255
baselines_compared: SMART (principled regularized optimization fine-tuning) [Jiang et al.]
LoRA and adversarial LoRA (LoRA-adv) adaptation of CLIP ViT-B/32 [Hu et al.; Ghiasvand et al.]

Prior worst-class/class-wise robustness methods discussed only in Related Work, NOT run as baselines by this paper: BAT, CFA, WAT, confusional spectral regularization [Sun et al.; Li & Liu; Jin et al.]
